metrics.title: DAU
metrics.why: Baseline health check; a >10% day-over-day drop warrants immediate incident review.

metrics.title: Trailing WAU
metrics.why: 7-day rolling uniques; removes weekday noise better than calendar WAU for trend calls.

metrics.title: Trailing MAU
metrics.why: 28-day rolling base; shows sustainable growth earlier than calendar MAU resets.

metrics.title: Total Online Time
metrics.why: Session volume aggregate; flat despite DAU growth reveals thinning per-user engagement.

metrics.title: Avg Session Length
metrics.why: Per-visit depth signal; sustained decline anticipates DAU churn by 1–2 weeks.

metrics.title: Matchmaking Success Rate
metrics.why: Queue UX health; below threshold means players abandon before a game starts.

metrics.title: Match Drop Rate
metrics.why: In-match quit signal; rising rate flags lag spikes, toxicity, or balance problems.

metrics.title: Avg Queue Wait
metrics.why: Long waits reduce daily match frequency and directly damage session-count retention.

metrics.title: Tutorial Completion Rate
metrics.why: Core onboarding KPI; low rate signals FTUE friction before players can activate.

metrics.title: Tutorial Starts
metrics.why: Raw new-player entry volume; sustained drop is the earliest acquisition slowdown signal.

metrics.title: Tutorial Completions
metrics.why: Activated-player count; sets the upper bound for revenue-eligible new cohorts.

metrics.title: Backflow Logins
metrics.why: Win-back volume; a lift following a campaign confirms re-engagement channel effectiveness.

metrics.title: Avg Onboarding Duration
metrics.why: Friction indicator; longer times correlate with early drop-off before first real match.

metrics.title: Avg Newbie Tutorial Score
metrics.why: Quality signal; consistently low scores flag confusing mechanics or weak tutorial design.

metrics.title: Tutorials Touched per Player
metrics.why: Breadth signal; measures how much of the onboarding surface new players actually explore.

metrics.title: Distinct Login Devices
metrics.why: Multi-device reach; growing count indicates successful cross-platform acquisition spread.

metrics.title: Total Gacha Diamond Spend
metrics.why: Primary soft-currency sink; tracks banner draw intensity and relative revenue contribution.

metrics.title: Gacha Participating Players
metrics.why: Banner reach metric; low share of DAU signals weak banner appeal or a price barrier.

metrics.title: Total Prop Consumption
metrics.why: Item economy sink depth; validates that game-loop rewards are converting into spend.

metrics.title: Total Currency Outflow
metrics.why: Aggregate spend pressure; compare to inflow ratio to detect inflationary imbalance.

metrics.title: Total Currency Inflow
metrics.why: Reward distribution scale; persistent excess over outflow signals economy inflation risk.

metrics.title: Ten-Pull Count
metrics.why: Bulk commitment gauge; rising ratio to single pulls indicates higher payer conviction.

metrics.title: Diamond-Fueled Pulls
metrics.why: Premium engagement proxy; separates paying players from free-currency gacha activity.

metrics.title: Net Weapon-Point Flow
metrics.why: Weapon economy health; persistent net drain without replenishment risks player frustration.

metrics.title: Daily Active Users (DAU)
metrics.why: Primary engagement pulse; day-on-day drops trigger LiveOps investigation and event response.

metrics.title: Monthly Active Users (MAU)
metrics.why: Top-line retention KPI; month-over-month change drives server-capacity and content-cadence decisions.

metrics.title: Avg Session Duration
metrics.why: Proxy for content depth; sustained decline signals fatigue or UX friction requiring event intervention.

metrics.title: Total Online Time
metrics.why: Aggregate engagement volume; confirms whether an event drove genuine play or merely extra logins.

metrics.title: Login Count
metrics.why: Session frequency metric; spikes correlate with events and drops flag server or UX regressions.

metrics.title: New In-Game Registrations
metrics.why: Daily player-inflow indicator; reflects server-launch appeal and content attractiveness independent of ad spend.

metrics.title: Active Server Count
metrics.why: Server population health signal; sustained low count versus expected indicates merge candidates or accelerating churn.

metrics.title: Monthly Recharging Users
metrics.why: Bridge metric linking engagement and monetization; flags when active base grows but paying share is shrinking.

metrics.title: Distinct New Registrations
metrics.why: Unique player inflow per period; normalises raw register count for users who create multiple roles.

metrics.title: Total User Base (Approx)
metrics.why: Fast HLL estimate of total acquired users; denominator for conversion-rate and paying-rate queries.

metrics.title: 30-Day Paying Conversion Rate
metrics.why: UA quality gate; compare across media sources and campaigns to identify channels worth scaling.

metrics.title: 30-Day LTV Total (VND)
metrics.why: Revenue return from recent installs; compute early ROAS per channel against UA spend.

metrics.title: ARPU (VND)
metrics.why: Revenue per user across the full base; benchmarks channel and campaign quality above payer-only metrics.

metrics.title: Paying Users (30-Day Window)
metrics.why: Recent converter volume; gap vs lifetime paying_users quantifies lapsing-payer accumulation.

metrics.title: Whale User Count
metrics.why: Tracks high-value user acquisition; whales drive disproportionate revenue so channel sourcing matters.

metrics.title: Lifetime Paying Users
metrics.why: Cumulative payer stock; rising gap versus paying_users_30d signals lapsing payers building up.

metrics.title: Daily Revenue (VND)
metrics.why: Real-time revenue pulse; day-on-day change surfaces event uplift or payment-gateway failures immediately.

metrics.title: Monthly Revenue (VND)
metrics.why: Primary revenue KPI for finance reporting; trend drives server-investment and content-budget decisions.

metrics.title: ARPPU (Revenue per Paying User)
metrics.why: Revenue intensity per payer; rise signals whale concentration, drop indicates promotional or mix dilution.

metrics.title: Transaction Count
metrics.why: Payment frequency signal; volume spike without proportional revenue rise indicates shift to smaller purchases.

metrics.title: Avg Revenue Per Transaction (ARPT)
metrics.why: Product mix and pricing efficiency indicator; tracks whether new packages shift average purchase size.

metrics.title: Monthly Paying Users
metrics.why: Active payer base per month; ratio against MAU derives the monthly paying rate for trend analysis.

metrics.title: Lifetime ARPPU (VND)
metrics.why: Lifetime monetisation intensity; separates casual spenders from dolphins and whales for targeted campaigns.

metrics.title: Monthly IAP Revenue (VND)
metrics.why: IAP-specific revenue stream; compare with web revenue to guide store-submission and payment-mix strategy.

metrics.title: Daily Active Users
metrics.why: Heartbeat metric; sharp day-over-day drops flag event fatigue, server issues, or missed liveops triggers.

metrics.title: Weekly Active Users
metrics.why: Smoothed engagement signal that filters daily noise; compare to DAU to derive stickiness ratio.

metrics.title: Trailing WAU
metrics.why: Real-time current-week player count; divide by trailing_mau for a live stickiness score.

metrics.title: Trailing MAU
metrics.why: Month-to-date reach denominator; baseline for stickiness, churn-rate, and payer-rate calculations.

metrics.title: Total Session Time
metrics.why: Engagement depth signal; a spike marks a compelling event, a drop reveals content exhaustion.

metrics.title: Active Server Count
metrics.why: Server health proxy; declining count signals player consolidation or migration pressure.

metrics.title: Lapsed Players This Month
metrics.why: Quantifies re-engagement urgency; feeds campaign audience sizing for win-back pushes.

metrics.title: Whale Count
metrics.why: Top-payer headcount; a small drop can represent outsized revenue risk requiring VIP outreach.

metrics.title: Installs
metrics.why: Top-of-funnel volume; anchors CPI and all downstream conversion rate calculations.

metrics.title: New Registered Users
metrics.why: Install-to-registration conversion; gap vs installs surfaces onboarding friction to fix.

metrics.title: ROAS
metrics.why: Primary UA efficiency verdict; channels below 1× drain budget, channels above 1× fund growth.

metrics.title: CPI (VND)
metrics.why: Unit acquisition cost; rank channels by CPI to quickly redirect budget to most efficient sources.

metrics.title: D1 Retention
metrics.why: First-day hook strength; strongest leading indicator of long-term retention and LTV potential.

metrics.title: D7 Retention
metrics.why: Mid-term engagement quality; key KPI for launch-phase health and core loop effectiveness.

metrics.title: D30 Retention Rate
metrics.why: Long-tail engagement anchor; low D30 despite decent D7 points to mid-game content gaps.

metrics.title: Ad Spend (VND)
metrics.why: Total spend denominator; required for computing CPI, CPC, CPM, and ROAS across all channels.
